buggy_code:
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
from sklearn.linear_model import Ridge
from sklearn.feature_selection import SelectKBest, f_regression

X = pd.read_csv('features.csv', index_col='date')
y = pd.read_csv('returns.csv', index_col='date')['target']

# Select features using FULL dataset including test data
selector = SelectKBest(f_regression, k=10)
X_selected = selector.fit_transform(X, y)

# Now split
X_train, X_test = X_selected[:800], X_selected[800:]
y_train, y_test = y.iloc[:800], y.iloc[800:]

model = Ridge()
model.fit(X_train, y_train)
preds = model.predict(X_test)
clean_code:
import pandas as pd
from sklearn.linear_model import Ridge
from sklearn.feature_selection import SelectKBest, f_regression
from sklearn.pipeline import Pipeline

X = pd.read_csv('features.csv', index_col='date')
y = pd.read_csv('returns.csv', index_col='date')['target']

# Split BEFORE feature selection
X_train, X_test = X.iloc[:800], X.iloc[800:]
y_train, y_test = y.iloc[:800], y.iloc[800:]

# Feature selection fitted on TRAINING data only
pipeline = Pipeline([
    ('selector', SelectKBest(f_regression, k=10)),
    ('model', Ridge())
])
pipeline.fit(X_train, y_train)
preds = pipeline.predict(X_test)
description: Feature selection performed on full dataset before train/test split.
explanation: SelectKBest.fit_transform(X, y) uses the full X and y to select the 10 best features. This means test-set target values influence which features are selected. The model then appears better on the test set because it was indirectly optimized using test labels during feature selection.
suggested_fix: Always perform feature selection inside a pipeline that is fitted only on training data.

issues.title: Feature selection uses full dataset including test labels
